name: Reykjavík
profile: Iceland's capital and largest city — government, finance, tech and startups, tourism, creative industries, and the University of Iceland and national hospital anchor most of the country's service economy.

name: Reykjanesbær
profile: The main town of the Reykjanes peninsula, beside Keflavík International Airport — aviation and logistics, tourism, geothermal energy nearby, fisheries, and one of Iceland's fastest-growing populations.

name: Akranes
profile: West Iceland's main town — a major fishing port and fish processing, heavy industry at nearby Grundartangi (aluminium and ferrosilicon), and close commuter links to the capital via the Hvalfjörður tunnel.

name: Ísafjörður
profile: The hub of the Westfjords — fisheries and a growing aquaculture sector, marine services, regional healthcare and education, and nature-based tourism.

name: Sauðárkrókur
profile: The largest town in the northwest, in Skagafjörður — agriculture and horse breeding, fish and food processing, biotechnology, and regional services.

name: Akureyri
profile: The capital of the north and Iceland's second urban centre — the University of Akureyri and a regional hospital, fisheries, manufacturing, tourism, and a broad services economy.

name: Egilsstaðir
profile: The centre of East Iceland — the region's transport hub, agriculture and forestry, power-intensive industry nearby (the Kárahnjúkar hydro plant and the Reyðarfjörður aluminium smelter), and regional services.

name: Selfoss
profile: The main town of the south — agriculture and food production, healthcare, commerce, a gateway to Golden Circle tourism, and a fast-growing commuter belt within reach of the capital.
